title: PM Work Identity-Joined Read Surface Integration Smoke
objective: Prove the six active identity-backed read-name fields materialize correctly against a real PostgreSQL-backed runtime path by exercising the existing GET handlers end to end with seeded identity and work rows, without adding endpoints, changing schemas, or authoring new SQL.
active_role: PM Identity-Joined Read Surface Integration Smoke Author
constrained_outputs: Real PostgreSQL-backed integration smoke coverage for the existing GET handlers covering work packages, assignments, execution issues, and progress snapshots
End-to-end evidence that the existing identity-backed *_name fields populate correctly from the six active relationships and remain None when the foreign key is null
Focused regression coverage proving packet-010a, packet-010b, packet-011f, and packet-012f surfaces remain stable after the integration smoke addition
No database-schema changes, no new endpoints, and no broader write-side runtime implementation
validation_steps: Confirm packet 012g touches only integration-smoke coverage for the existing GET handlers and does not add new endpoints or broaden mutation scope
Confirm the six existing identity-backed *_name fields materialize correctly against a real PostgreSQL-backed runtime path and remain None when the foreign key is null
Confirm no new SQL, request schemas, or schema changes are introduced and packet 012g remains integration-smoke only
Confirm packet-010a model tests, packet-010b API tests, packet-011f project-write tests, and packet-012f joined-read tests still pass after the smoke coverage addition
sql_permission_note: Packet 012g is integration-smoke runtime coverage only. No new SQL, schema changes, migration edits, or write-surface expansion are authorized.
